stemHtml:
An aircraft must make an unplanned diversion and the pilot wishes to climb as little as possible. The highest obstacle on the route is a mountain with an elevation of 4 430 ft, and the pilot requires 2 000 ft of vertical clearance above this. The local QNH at an airport of elevation 2 420 ft is 994 hPa, OAT +9ºC. Calculate the minimum usable flight level in order to clear the mountain by the specified clearance.

Use 1 hPa = 30 ft
